title: Scrub untrusted input before assembly
description: User and external input is sanitized and length-bounded before it is placed into the prompt, not after.
body: Use when external text enters the prompt pipeline. Do not assemble the prompt and clean it afterward, and avoid passing unbounded or control-character-laden input through. Actions: normalize and bound input length, strip or neutralize control sequences and instruction-like delimiters, and label the cleaned content as data before assembly. Validation signals include a test that oversized or delimiter-laden input is bounded and neutralized before reaching the template. Risk boundary: scrubbing after assembly leaves a window where raw input shapes the prompt; scrub at the boundary and treat unscrubbed input reaching the template as a blocking finding.
